objective: Validate the offline CPU-matrix-driven MMAPI/VPI/NVENC device-side warp path and define its claim boundary before using it as a stage demo.

allowed: MMAPI/NvBufSurface scratch-buffer path can run VPI CUDA warp before NVENC
inverse matrix is the current correct direction for this device-side VPI path
offline matrix-driven device warp and encode path is validated as a stage boundary
device-side VPI warp timing is approximately 1.25 to 1.42 ms per 1080p frame in the recorded run
forbidden: CPU stabilized output is pixel-equivalent to device output
CPU crop, dynamic zoom, Lanczos resize, and sharpen are fully reproduced on device

validated_observations.forward_matrix_result: forward matrix produced about 30% black area in the sampled sanity frames and is not the default direction
validated_observations.inverse_matrix_result: inverse matrix sampled sanity frames have black_ratio around 0.028 to 0.029 and normal mean/std
validated_observations.triptych_cpu_vs_device: local review-video panel comparison over 120 frames shows mean_abs_center_avg=37.033757 and p95_abs_center_avg=138.416667, so this is not CPU-output equivalence
validated_observations.panel_video_cpu_vs_device: local panel-video comparison over 120 frames shows mean_abs_center_avg=39.751371 and p95_abs_center_avg=143.175417
validated_observations.frame_timeline_gap: cpu_stabilize.py exports 119 matrices for a 120-frame output; the device path currently needs a 120-row matrix CSV with an explicit first-frame row
validated_observations.jetson_ab_result: aligned_identity_first loaded 120 rows but worsened CPU/device diff; post_geometry_identity_first is current best with mean_abs_center_avg vs CPU = 30.241568
validated_observations.codec_color_floor: identity transcode already has mean_abs_center_avg=25.664099 vs source, so raw pixel diff has a high codec/colorspace floor
validated_observations.vpi_border_interp_boundary: VPI PerspectiveWarp only accepts VPI_BORDER_ZERO; Catmull-Rom interpolation was tested and rejected because it was slower and worse than linear
validated_observations.run_quirk: do not use --stats for the current multivideo_transcode matrix path; it printed success but produced 0-byte output in this session
validated_observations.reason_for_gap: device path currently uses VPI_BORDER_ZERO and linear interpolation and does not reproduce CPU post-processing such as dynamic zoom, fixed crop/resize, Lanczos interpolation, or sharpen

required_outputs: stage report with allowed and forbidden claims
frozen_variables: Python appsink/appsrc is not the active path
forward matrix is not the default device direction
next_alignment_tasks: pull or preserve raw CPU/device output videos locally when Jetson auth is available
compare raw CPU stabilized video and device output directly instead of through encoded triptych panels
use device_matrices_inverse_post_geometry_identity_first.csv as the current best device-side candidate
investigate remaining parity gap by isolating border workaround and colorspace/encoding differences
do not continue Catmull-Rom interpolation; measured slower and worse
decide whether to implement device-side crop/zoom/sharpen parity or keep the result as a warp/encode demo only

recovery_actions.jetson_ssh_auth_blocker: Use existing local logs and review video for boundary documentation, then ask user for Jetson password/key only if direct raw-video comparison is required.
recovery_actions.cpu_device_diff_too_large_for_equivalenc: Do not promote to CPU-equivalent demo. Either add device post-processing parity or keep it as a device-side warp/encode boundary result.
recovery_actions.matrix_direction_unclear: Use forward-vs-inverse black-ratio sanity first; do not default to forward matrix.
